name: Q1871516
abstract: Louis Barbarin (nickname Lil Barb; October 24, 1902 – May 12, 1997) was a New Orleans jazz drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing piano and Louis Barbarin was playing drums... And we were doing like “Sensation Rag”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Louis Barbarin’s wife, Marie Philip, Louis used to go to hang with her sister... They said, 'I want you a little girl to meet.' ... So they send for me next Sunday or a couple of Sundays later. I went and I seen this fine girl they tell me they want me to meet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: And how I met him, his Aunt, Louis Barbarin's wife, and her sister, she said to me, 'You know something? I've got a nice little boy I want you to meet.' ... So Sunday after church, Gladys and II went to his Aunt's house, and they introduced me to him.
